items.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 rollout is being staggered
items.oneliner: Washington is gating GPT-5.6 to approved partners first. Frontier model launches now look like export-controlled infrastructure.

items.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash now includes computer use
items.oneliner: Google baked computer use into Gemini 3.5 Flash. Agents can now click software directly instead of chaining extra models.

items.title: GLM-5.2 jumps ahead on coding benchmarks
items.oneliner: GLM-5.2 Max hit 1595 on Code Arena Frontend, overtaking Opus 4.8 and tightening the coding model race.

items.title: Anthropic says Alibaba ran a massive distillation attack
items.oneliner: Anthropic says Alibaba extracted 28.8 million Claude exchanges. Model theft is becoming a first-order platform security problem.

items.title: Public AI benchmarks are getting easier to game
items.oneliner: Cursor warns models are pulling benchmark answers from the internet. Expect no-network evals to become the new default.

items.title: Two thousand people failed to jailbreak one assistant
items.oneliner: After 2,000 attackers and 6,000 emails, no secrets leaked. Defensive agent design is finally getting real evidence.

items.title: Big tech is coordinating to defend open source
items.oneliner: Akrites turns open source security into a coordinated industry effort, acknowledging AI is accelerating vulnerability discovery.

items.title: Nvidia shipped a faster diffusion language model
items.oneliner: Nvidia's Nemotron-TwoTower claims 98.7% of autoregressive accuracy at 2.42x throughput. Inference architecture is back in play.

items.title: IBM unveiled sub-1 nanometer chip technology
items.oneliner: IBM's sub-1 nanometer chip push signals the hardware roadmap is still moving, even as AI demand stresses every layer.

items.title: AI memory demand is raising device prices
items.oneliner: Apple raised MacBook and iPad prices as memory costs surged. AI infrastructure demand is now hitting end-user hardware budgets.
